inclusion.I2: Human partcipants performing a problem-solving or reasoning task.
inclusion.I3: Includes at least one group of healthy adults (18-65).

inclusion.I5: Task-based fMRI with group-level whole-brain analyses.
inclusion.I6: Problem-solving task involving multi-step reasoning (e.g. math, verbal, logical, visuospatial, physics).
inclusion.I7: At least one group of healthy adults (18-65), reproted separately if mixed.
inclusion.I8: Reports peak coordinates (MNI or Talairach) for problem-solving contrasts.

exclusion.E7: Simple tasks without clear problem-solving demands.
exclusion.E8: Between-group contrasts only (no healthy-group task effect).

inclusion.GLOBAL_I1: Sample- The analysis must be on a healthy adult participants (alone, not a clinical or special group, or a comparison between healthy and clinical/special groups).
inclusion.GLOBAL_I2: Whole-brain evidence- The analysis must be from a group-level, univariate whole-brain (voxelwise) task-evoked statistical map (e.g., main effect or within-group contrast) that is from the healthy/control group and is generalizable to the healthy adult population.

exclusion.GLOBAL_E2: Seed-based connectivity, effective connectivity (e.g., PPI, DCM), or resting-state analyses (e.g., ICA, PLE, Hurst exponents)
exclusion.GLOBAL_E3: Between-group contrasts or interactions (e.g., subgroup vs subgroup, patient vs control)
